skill.name: gene-structure-mapper
skill.description: Visualize gene exon-intron structures with support for domain annotation and mutation marking on diagrams.
skill.summary: Gene structure mapper for exon-intron visualization with annotation support
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 44 static analysis findings are false positives after manual evaluation. The 28 'shell backtick execution' alerts match markdown fenced code blocks showing example python commands in SKILL.md, not actual command injection. Six 'system reconnaissance' alerts match descriptive text about parameter configuration and test cases. Three 'weak cryptographic algorithm' alerts are false matches against non-cryptographic text, including scripts/main.py:18 which is an argparse description string. Two 'path traversal' alerts match text in a security checklist documenting protective measures. Two 'hardcoded URL' alerts match a GitHub source attribution link. The only real risk factor is external command execution (python script invocation), which is the intended function of the skill. The main script (scripts/main.py) is a benign 28-line argparse wrapper with no network access, no file writes, and no external dependencies. The skill is safe to publish with a low residual risk rating.

content.user_title: Map and visualize gene structures with exon-intron diagrams
content.value_statement: Researchers and educators need clear gene structure diagrams to understand exon-intron organization and communicate findings. This skill maps gene symbols to visual exon-intron representations with optional domain annotations and mutation markers.
actual_capabilities: Generate gene structure diagrams showing exon-intron organization from gene symbols
Accept standard HGNC gene symbols as input such as TP53 and BRCA1
Support multiple output formats including SVG, PNG, and PDF
Mark specific mutation positions on gene structure diagrams
Include protein domain annotations on gene visualizations
Save rendered diagrams to a specified output file path
limitations: The packaged script generates placeholder text output rather than actual rendered diagrams
Requires a workspace with Python 3.10+ for script execution
Does not connect to external gene databases for real-time data retrieval
Full diagram rendering requires additional library installation beyond the base script

use_cases.title: Visualize gene structure for research publications
use_cases.description: Bioinformatics researchers can generate exon-intron diagrams of target genes for inclusion in manuscripts, presentations, and grant applications.

use_cases.title: Teach gene structure and splicing concepts
use_cases.description: Educators can create gene structure visualizations to help students understand exon-intron organization and the effects of alternative splicing.

use_cases.title: Annotate mutations on clinical gene diagrams
use_cases.description: Clinical geneticists can visualize mutation positions relative to gene exons when analyzing and reporting genetic variants for patient cases.

prompt_templates.title: Basic gene structure map
prompt_templates.prompt: Map the exon-intron structure of the TP53 gene.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user exploring a single gene

prompt_templates.title: Custom format output
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a gene structure diagram for BRCA1 showing all exons in SVG format.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs a specific output format for a publication

prompt_templates.title: Mutations and domain annotations
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a gene structure visualization for the CFTR gene with domain annotations and mark the following mutation positions: 250, 1000, 1500.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher analyzing mutation locations relative to gene domains

prompt_templates.title: Batch gene analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Map the gene structures for a list of genes: TP53, BRCA1, EGFR, and MYC. Save each diagram as a separate SVG file in the output directory.

output_examples.input: Show the exon-intron structure of TP53.
output_examples.output: Gene structure diagram for TP53 with 11 exons displayed. Exons are numbered 1 through 11 and connected by intronic regions. Untranslated regions are marked at both the 5-prime and 3-prime ends.

output_examples.input: Mark mutation R175H on the TP53 gene diagram in SVG format.
output_examples.output: TP53 structure diagram with 11 exons rendered in SVG format. Mutation R175H is highlighted on exon 5 with a red annotation marker and a detailed callout box.

best_practices: Verify the gene symbol follows HGNC naming conventions before generating a diagram
Confirm your workflow tools support the selected output format (SVG, PNG, or PDF)
Review the generated diagram for correct exon numbering and annotation placements
anti_patterns: Using unofficial gene aliases instead of standard HGNC symbols may produce incorrect or empty results
Requesting visualization for genes without known exon-intron boundaries in the reference data
Expecting real-time genomic database queries that the skill does not support

faq.question: What gene symbols does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill accepts standard HGNC gene symbols such as TP53, BRCA1, and CFTR.

faq.question: What output formats are available?
faq.answer: The skill supports SVG, PNG, and PDF output formats for gene structure diagrams.

faq.question: Can I mark multiple mutations on one diagram?
faq.answer: Yes, you can provide a comma-separated list of mutation positions using the --mutations parameter.

faq.question: Does this skill connect to external gene databases?
faq.answer: No. The skill works with gene symbols you provide and does not fetch data from external databases.

faq.question: Can I save the output to a specific file?
faq.answer: Yes, use the --output or -o parameter to specify a file path for the generated diagram.

faq.question: What is the difference between exons and introns?
faq.answer: Exons are coding regions retained in the final mRNA. Introns are non-coding regions spliced out during RNA processing.
